summary: This week in AI news, significant advancements were made in various sectors, including the introduction of new AI models, hardware, and applications. OpenAI released a light-up keyboard for Codex, while NVIDIA introduced new Jetson Thor computers for mainstream robotics and edge AI. Several companies, such as Applied Computing and Emergent, secured funding to further develop their AI technologies.
insight: The increasing focus on AI safety and security is a common thread throughout the news, with companies like OpenAI and NVIDIA working to improve the robustness and reliability of their models, and governments taking steps to regulate the use of AI.
highlights: OpenAI released a light-up keyboard for Codex, designed to monitor multiple agentic threads at a glance.
NVIDIA introduced new Jetson Thor computers to advance mainstream robotics and edge AI.
Applied Computing raised $20M to build a foundation AI model for the oil, gas, and petrochemical industry.

articles.description: OpenAI outlines a “reverse federalism” approach to AI governance, where state laws help build a national framework for safe, democratic AI.
